name: Creamy Curry Dip
directions: In a medium glass bowl, combine all ingredients. Mix well and chill in refrigerator for at least 3 hours. Cook time will be time for chilling in refrigerator.
Serve dip with your favorite assorted vegetables.

name: Cool Seven Layer Dip
directions: In a 9-inch pie plate, combine beans and hot sauce; stir until well blended.
Spread mixture evenly over the bottom of pie plate. Add taco mix to sour cream and stir until well blended; spread over bean mixture.
Layer dip with cheese, tomatoes, avocado, green onions and olives.
Cover and chill 1 hour; serve with chips.

name: Layered Nacho Dip
directions: Spread beans in a rectangular casserole dish (9x13-inch).  You could use chili instead, as well.
Mix sour cream and cream cheese together (use a hand mixer or immersion blender).  Spread over beans or chili.
Spread salsa over sour cream/cream cheese layer.
Sprinkle cheese over salsa.
Add peppers, tomatoes, and green onions in layers.  Top with jalapeño peppers or olives, if using.  You can also put these on the side, so your guests can choose to add them or not.
Chill until serving time.  Serve with Nacho Chips.

name: Jalapeño-Artichoke Dip
directions: Preheat oven to 350°F.
Put cream cheese, jalapeño peppers*, marinated artichoke hears, and blue cheese crumbles in a large bowl and mix well.
Transfer mixture into a 9x9 square baking dish.
Bake at 350°F for 25 to 30 minutes or until bubbly.
Stir well and serve with sliced baguettes, vegetables, crackers, or chips.
*Add half the chopped jalapeño peppers or the whole can depending on how spicy you want it.

name: Maryland Dill Dip
directions: Mix yogurt and mayonnaise.
Mix in dry ingredients, but reserve 1 tbs. onion crisps for topping.
Refrigerate for at least an hour to allow flavors to mingle.

name: Easy Cheesy Corn Dip (Mild)
directions: Mix together the sour cream and mayo, then add the red pepper flakes, onion flakes and cumin.
Then add the cheese and the corn and stir until well coated.
Refrigerate overnight so the flavors blend.
Serve with Frito's Scoops.

name: Spicy Ranch Dip
directions: Mix all ingredients in a bowl.
Cover and refrigerate until ready to use (this does get hotter with age so you may want to use less jalapenos).

name: Simple Onion Dip (For Appetizers)
directions: Blend the two ingredients with a spoon or fork.
Allow the dip to marinate in the refrigerator for 2 hours before serving with veggies or chips.

name: Artichoke Feta Dip (Hot)
directions: Preheat oven to 350°.
Mix all ingredients together (except paprika).
Place mixture in a pie pan or casserole.
sprinkle top with paprika.
Bake 20- 25 minutes or until slightly browned.
Serve with pita chips, bread, tortilla chips or crackers.

name: Pizza Dip Surprise
directions: Mix together all ingredients EXCEPT 3/4 cup of the shredded cheese. (Pizza Toppings can be left as big or cut as small as you like).
Spread into a casserole or baking or oven proof dish. The thinner the dip is spread the quicker it will cook.
Sprinkle the remaining shredded cheese on top.
Bake @ 350 until cheese on top is melted (about 20 minutes) and then turn on the broiler for a few seconds (or minutes depending on the broiler) to brown the top a little.
Serve with the tortilla chips.
You should wait a few minutes for it to cool down before you eat it but we usually dig right in and burn our mouths because we just can't wait!

name: Creamy Fruit Dip
directions: Mix all ingredients with electric mixer.
Chill before serving.
Serve with your favorite sliced fruit for dipping.
